canonical_definition: The extent to which a benchmark update prevents a contaminated LLM from gaining an unfair advantage due to prior exposure to the original benchmark, ensuring that performance remains consistent regardless of contamination.
expert_removed_reason: data property / engineering — not a construct of LLM ability
papers: Automated Benchmark Generation for Repository-Level Coding Tasks
The Emperor’s New Clothes in Benchmarking? A Rigorous Examination of Mitigation Strategies for LLM Benchmark Data Contamination
